patch.value:
## Context

schema.unknown_field_policy is:
- Defined: src/types.ts:400 (RuntimeUnknownFieldPolicy)
- Validated: src/core/store/settings-validator.ts:285
- Honored: src/core/item/item-format.ts:89 (unknownFieldPolicy: options.schema.unknown_field_policy)
- Default: 'allow' (src/core/shared/constants.ts:290)
- Listed in serializeSettings orderObject call (src/core/store/settings.ts:789)

But not in:
- CONFIG_KEY_VALUES (src/cli/commands/config.ts:44-92)
- NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS (src/core/config/nested-settings.ts)

Adding it to NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS with kind='string' and choices=['allow','warn','strict'] follows the established pattern.

## Acceptance criteria

- pm config set schema_unknown_field_policy allow|warn|strict updates the setting
- pm config get schema_unknown_field_policy returns the current value
- pm config list includes the key
- CONFIGURATION.md documents this setting with use-case examples

## Evidence

- src/types.ts:400 (RUNTIME_UNKNOWN_FIELD_POLICY_VALUES = ['allow','warn','strict'])
- src/core/store/settings-validator.ts:285 (vLiteral validation)
- src/core/config/nested-settings.ts:54-206 (NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS — missing this key)
Expose schema.unknown_field_policy via pm config set schema-unknown-field-policy

schema.unknown_field_policy (allow/warn/strict) controls how item I/O handles metadata keys not in the runtime field registry. It is defined in RuntimeSchemaSettings (src/types.ts:400), has defaults in constants.ts (allow), and is honored in item-format.ts, but it is not settable via pm config set. Projects that want to catch typos in field names (strict mode) or surface warnings (warn mode) must hand-edit settings.json.

- pm config set schema_unknown_field_policy allow|warn|strict updates the setting
- pm config get schema_unknown_field_policy returns the current value
- pm config list includes the key
- CONFIGURATION.md documents this setting with use-case examples
